title: Steady Before the Stage
intention: To turn pre-performance nerves into grounded, present energy and walk out ready.

lines.text: Welcome. Take a moment here before you go on.
You don't have to calm down completely. You just have to get steady.
Plant your feet flat on the floor.
Feel the ground holding all your weight.
Now notice the buzz in your body. The fast pulse, the energy in your chest.
That's not fear. That's fuel.
Take a slow breath in through your nose.
And let it out, longer than you took it in.
Again. Breathe in, steady.
Breathe out, and drop your shoulders.
One more. Fill low, into your belly.
Release, and let your jaw soften.
Let the energy stay. You're just giving it somewhere to go.
Feel it move down through your legs and into the floor.
Now bring your attention to one simple thing. The first note. The first line. The first step.
You don't need the whole thing yet. Just the start.
Picture yourself beginning, calm and clear.
Your hands know what to do. Your voice knows. Let them.
Take one more full breath, and let it sharpen you.
Lift your eyes. Lengthen your spine.
You are ready. The energy is yours now.
Go on. Step out, and begin.
